lines: A note above the largest row of all. The hand is pressed hard enough to tear the page in places.

'The loud ones. Not sick. Not thieves. They argued a tithe at a gate, or named the wrong man at a table, or printed a question someone above me did not want answered. The cart-papers called them seditious and the order was the same as it was for the plague.'
'I filled more wall with these than with the sick. Think on that. A house built to hold a contagion, and most of what I buried in it was people who would not stop talking.'
